description_long: This is a roughly hewn stone sculpture with a weathered, irregular surface and a predominantly pale, off-white coloration. The front features a partially discernible human face, with deep-set eyes and a prominent brow, while the top and sides are adorned with abstract, organic forms that may suggest animalistic or mythological motifs. The stone is pitted and uneven, with visible signs of erosion and age, and some areas show darker discoloration, likely from environmental exposure. Metal bands or supports are attached to the sides, possibly for stabilization or display purposes. The back of the object is less detailed, with a rough, uncarved surface that contrasts with the more intricate front. Overall, the object exudes an ancient, monumental quality, reminiscent of urban archaeological finds or public art relics.
description: A weathered, pale stone sculpture featuring a partially carved human face and abstract forms, with metal supports and a rough, eroded surface. The object has an ancient, monumental appearance.
description_view_0: The front view reveals a partially carved human face with deep-set features and a textured, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This side view highlights an abstract, animal-like motif with a prominent eye and visible metal support band.
description_view_2: The rear view shows a rough, uncarved stone surface with minimal detail and darker discoloration.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays another abstract form, similar to view 1, with a focus on the stone's irregular texture and additional metal support.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a paved or landscaped area
attribute_pattern: Irregular, weathered surface with some carved facial and abstract motifs; no regular geometric pattern, but distinct areas of erosion and discoloration.
attribute_weathering: Heavily weathered with pitting, erosion, and dark stains indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with modern urban elements but may blend with other stone features or landscaping; metal supports visually separate it from natural stones.
